{"key_event":"Independence and UNIP state centralization","reasoning":"Independence, Africanization, emergency powers, suppression of challengers, and nationalizations pushed Zambia from a strong late-colonial state toward a more centralized party-state with greater coercive and economic reach. Civil society remained substantial through unions, urban workers, ANC/UNIP organization, and elections, but its autonomy was increasingly reduced by UNIP dominance, detention powers, centralization against regional institutions, and incorporation into the state.","society_change":-0.5,"society_power":6.0,"state_change":0.8,"state_power":8.3}
